zh.Frittenden,Kent,,England: 英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登
zh.Saint Mary Churchyard, Frittenden, Tunbr: 英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登
zh.lifeSummary: 當Stephen Guillaume Payne於1450年出生在英國肯特福利頓登時，他的父親John Payne30歲，母親Joane Webb27歲。他於1478年與Joanne Alice Webb在英國肯特福利頓登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1505年10月21日在他的家鄉享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Stephen Guillaume Payne于1450年出生在英国肯特福利頓登时，他的父亲John Payne30岁，母亲Joane Webb27岁。他于1478年与Joanne Alice Webb在英国肯特福利頓登结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1505年10月21日在他的家乡享年55岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特福利頓登。

nameMeanings.name: Payne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen (from Latin Paganus), a fairly common personal name among Normans. It derived from a word that originally meant ‘villager, rustic’, later ‘heathen’, but it had doubtless lost these connotations in its use as a late medieval personal name. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

History: Thomas Payne, who was a freeman of Plymouth Colony in 1639, was the founder of a large American family, which included Robert Treat Paine (1731–1814), one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. See also   Paine  .

variants.name: Payn
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Payen
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Pagen, Paien, from Latin Paganus, from paganus ‘country-dweller’, a derivative of pagus ‘of or relating to the countryside’, possibly given to a child not yet baptized (because of its later sense of ‘pagan, heathen’). Alternatively, a nickname for a country-born person. This surname is also found in the West Indies (mainly Haiti) and Mauritius.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Pagan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Angel, Carlos, Rafael, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Julio.

Spanish and Catalan: Castilianized form (Pagán) of Catalan Pagà, from the Late Latin personal name Paganus, which as a derivative of Latin pagus ‘outlying village’ originally meant ‘villager’ (see Payne ). Alternatively, in some cases, a topographic name from Catalan pagà ‘heath’ (from the same Latin word pagus).
English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Pai(e)n, Pagen, from Late Latin Paganus (see Payne , compare 1 above).

variants.name: Paynes
meanings: English: variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paine
meanings: English: variant of   Payne  .

History: The author of the republican treatise The Rights of Man, Thomas Paine (1737–1809), left England for North America in the mid 1770s, where he became involved in the movement that led to independence. His pamphlet of 1776, Common Sense, influenced the Declaration of Independence and furnished some of the arguments justifying it.

variants.name: Pank
meanings: English (Norfolk): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Pank(e). It could be a shortened form of Pancok, which is probably a pet form of Pane (see Payne ).
Sorbian: nickname from a diminutive of pan ‘mister, lord, landlord’ (see Pan ). Compare Panke .

variants.name: Pane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Salvatore, Aldo, Carmela, Carmine, Ciro, Concetta, Enzo, Giuseppe, Mario, Armando, Claudio, Macario, Remigio.

Italian: from pane ‘bread’, used as a metonymic occupational name for a baker or as a nickname meaning ‘good thing’, and also as a personal name. This surname is also found in southern France and Corsica.
English: variant of   Payne  .

variants.name: Panes
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Payne , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Pannell
meanings: English (southeastern):
possibly from Middle English panel, panel(l)e ‘cloth, saddle-cloth, saddle’ or ‘pane (of glass)’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such items.
from the Middle English personal name Paynel (Old French Payenel, Latin Paganellus), a pet form of Payne (from Latin Paganus).

variants.name: Penson
meanings: English:
patronymic from the Middle English female personal name Penn, a pet form of Pernel, or perhaps from the Middle English personal name Payne + -son. See Penn , Parnell .

habitational name from Penson in North Huish (Devon), which derives from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Payn(e) (see Payne ) + Middle English toun ‘village, town, settlement’ (Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’).

nameMeanings.name: Stephen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , preserving the vowels of the Greek name.

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , reflecting the normal pronunciation of the name in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Stephen Guillaume Payne was born in 1450, in Frittenden, Kent, England, his father, John Payne, was 30 and his mother, Joane Webb, was 27. He married Joanne Alice Webb in 1478, in Frittenden, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 21 October 1505, in his hometown, at the age of 55, and was buried in Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Stephen Guillaume Payne est né en 1450, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, son père, John Payne, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Joane Webb, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Joanne Alice Webb en 1478, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 21 octobre 1505, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Stephen Guillaume Payne 1450 in Frittenden, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Payne 30 und seine Mutter Joane Webb 27. Er hat 1478 in Frittenden, Kent, England Joanne Alice Webb geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 21. Oktober 1505 im Alter von 55 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Stephen Guillaume Payne è nato il nel 1450 nel Frittenden, Kent, England, il suo padre, John Payne, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Joane Webb, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Joanne Alice Webb nel 1478, nel Frittenden, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 21 ottobre 1505, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Frittenden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Stephen Guillaume Payneが1450年にイギリスケントフリッテンデンで生まれた時，父John Payneは30歳，母Joane Webbは27歳であった。1478年にイギリスケントフリッテンデンでJoanne Alice Webbと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1505年10月21日に故郷で55歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントフリッテンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Stephen Guillaume Payne(이)가 1450년 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Payne(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane Webb(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1478년 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 Joanne Alice Webb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1505년 10월 21일 그의 고향에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Stephen Guillaume Payne nasceu em 1450, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Payne, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Joane Webb, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Joanne Alice Webb em 1478, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 21 de outubro de 1505, em sua cidade natal, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Stephen Guillaume Payne родился в 1450 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени John Payne было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Webb, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanne Alice Webb в 1478 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 21 октября 1505 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Stephen Guillaume Payne nació en 1450, en Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, John Payne, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Joane Webb, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Joanne Alice Webb en 1478, en Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 21 de octubre de 1505, en su pueblo, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
